title: framing.rs: KissReader::feed silently discards excess input during overflow
description:
file: rust/lichen-kiss/src/framing.rs
lines: 384-386

When KissReader buffer overflows and is shifted, remaining input data that exceeds buffer capacity is silently discarded:

```rust
let to_copy2 = remaining.len().min(self.buffer.len() - self.len);
self.buffer[self.len..self.len + to_copy2].copy_from_slice(&remaining[..to_copy2]);
self.len += to_copy2;
```

If remaining.len() > (buffer.len() - self.len), excess bytes are dropped with no indication. This can cause frame corruption when high-throughput data streams fill the 4KB buffer.

Impact: Silent data loss in high-throughput scenarios, potential frame corruption.

Fix: Either return an error/result indicating truncation, or track stats for discarded bytes.
